title: tormentilla recta
intro: Tormentilla recta, also known as pot marigold or yellow woundwort, is a perennial plant in the family Ranunculaceae, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. Tormentilla recta is rich in flavonoids, particularly saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties and provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Tormentilla recta can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
